text: Statue cruises are fine, I would give them 5 stars for what you get for the buck. However, there is an insane number of scam sellers in the area that pretend to be StatueCruises employees and hawk on tourists. They have jackets that match the StatueCruises colors, they have officially looking employee cards on their necks and they even say they work for the StatueCruises company. Except they are not. They jump on you as soon as you leave the metro and lie that tickets are sold out for the day and try to sell you express tours. Then they take you to shuttle buses that leave for boats docket somewhere in Brooklyn, which you realize only after paying. A total scam. We were smart enough not to pay, but saw lots of tourists that did and were being boarded into the shuttles. When we declined their offer we were shouted at, harassed and intimidated. This was in the middle of a day. And the worst part was that the police were present just around the corner but did nothing to stop them. This thing needs to be sorted out, otherwise it is a big shame on the company and the NEW YORK CITY as a whole.
name: Statue City Cruises Battery Park/ Liberty Island
category: Tourist attraction||Boat tour agency||Ferry service||Historical landmark||Sculpture||Tour agency

text: Had an enjoyable time here. Went to the museum as a last second idea but turned out to be the best choice for the evening. They had a free salsa concert in the lobby and many couples and friends were dancing. They sold cheap drinks and food to go along with the event. As for the museum itself it's less than $20 entry per adult but this night it was free to roam about. There are five floors and each offered interesting exhibits from around the world and time periods. The vast space between each set allows you to better focus on each piece before moving on to the next. Would recommend if you have at least an hour to kill.
name: Brooklyn Museum

text: Beautiful wildlife refuge! It’s hard to imagine in a place like NY, but this gem is an oasis nestled among the hustle and bustle of it all. text: The Museum is a small gem that always surprises! The Ronald Lockett retrospective, Fever Within, was totally moving! His biography so tragic to have died so young is reflected in some of the work but so is his compassion. You really feel the raw emotion of his work in the subject matter he chose. The companion exhibition that the Folk Art Museum put together using one of Lockett's most compelling works ONCE SOMETHING HAS LIVED IT CAN NEVER REALLY DIE was thought provoking and wonderful and a dramatic way to utilize works from the collection that related to this powerful theme. Additionally they have the best museum shop in New York!

text: Considering how small this zoo is, it does a great job! The zoo itself is stunningly gorgeous, and the exhibits are pretty too. I thought the reptile house was pretty neat, but it was pretty hot and stuffy in there (that makes sense given their habitat, just giving visitors a heads up). The rainforest exhibit is incredible too, featuring various birds that fly right over your head! My favorite animal was the red panda, mostly because my hair matches their fur, but also because they’re just so adorable. It would have been cool if the grizzly bears weren’t sleeping because they have a cool exhibit, but that’s alright. If you have young kids, or want a change in scenery you should check out the petting zoo. All in all, a fun, little zoo that is especially good for families with young kids!

text: If I could give them 0 stars, I would. This was the most horrible customer service experience I have ever had. After paying $300 for white glove delivery, the company’s driver drove onto my lawn. After spinning their wheels into our lawn and damaging it, they had to call a tow truck company to have their truck removed. The tow truck driver noted that their tire was flat. On top of that, they delivered a damaged couch. The driver told me that I should keep it until they could deliver a replacement. Upon notifying the company of the damaged product and damage to my lawn, France and Son Customer Support Supervisor Tyler told us we needed to pay their tow truck bill before they would even talk to us about the damaged couch they delivered. There wasn't even an opportunity to discuss the situation or explain our side of the story. They refused to deal with the defective product or our customer service situation until we addressed this truck situation. This is the first time I've been extorted by a retail business but it will certainly be the last. It has been six weeks and the company has not returned any of our calls or emails. In the last correspondence, they stated that their driver did what he was told to do. But, of course we never told their driver to drive onto our lawn. Further, their driver took the liberty to drive the entire length of my driveway, which I never asked them to do nor did they need to do that. They have been completely unreasonable. This experience has been a complete waste of time, money and a great deal of stress. We just want to return this couch but we've been abandoned. This is a hard lesson in only making purchases from reputable stores like DWR, Crate and Barrel, and CB2. Avoid this company at all costs.
name: France and Son
category: Furniture store||Art gallery||Bedroom furniture store||Furniture accessories||Furniture maker||Furniture rental service||Home goods store||Kitchen furniture store||Lighting store||Office furniture store

text: I have yet to go to an haunted house that has scared me, and this was just another to add to the list of not scary houses. After reading up and feeding into the hype I was truly nervous at first about the whole experience. I have never been to a house that separates you from your group and only allows small groups in at a time. I bought 8 pm tickets and although it says to show up 15 minutes prior I immediately realized that should have been more like 45 minutes to an hour, since the wait was more than an hour to get in. The line process which should seem simple was actually very complicated. You have to wait in line to check your bag (which is 5 dollars), just to be told by someone that you have to go to the back of the line that you just waited an hour in. Be prepared for the fact that this is not the most secure bag check. Also, If you were expecting to leave your phone in your purse good luck, because you are forced to bring your phone into the house if you purchased tickets online. When I got in the house there were groups of 4 or five being let into the house at a time. Although they separate you many times throughout the house it was fairly easy to find your party again later on. One thing I have to give you credit on is the fact that I was not only hit by one of your prop doors but the actor themself( not on purpose of course) because there were sections that were a bit too tight to have someone jumping out. Definitely was not expecting that! You should add that one to your rules, "props and actors allowed to touch and hit you, but you can't touch them". (Seriously not upset about it just making light of it). In addition the actors seemed a bit tired by the time I got in, or at least I hope they were tired and this was not the norm. The scares were not there, just loud screaming that you can hear ahead of you that ruins the effect. In fact, I even said out loud a few times that the effect is ruined when you continue to scream at each person walking through the doorway. It eventually started to become a bit annoying. For me, I think the best part was the slide... that says a ton. IMO, they don't keep you separated long enough to make it scary. Things are more confusing than scary since you don't know which way to go 75 percent of the time since. Nothing is marked or clear. It was very short and I'm glad it was a groupon deal. . I get it's your first year in nyc, but I think you dropped the ball. I gave bane a chance for a few reasons. First I know some former workers who highly recommended it Secondly it was convenient for my group being I was already going to be in the area that weekend. Thirdly, I had already been to blood manor and wasn't impressed with what they had to offer. Lastly was the groupon offer, which made it a deal over your competitors. With all of those factors I paid to be scared but was disappointed. Hopefully you do better next year when you've had more time to think it through. In its current form, I would only recommend this to those that are easily scared and have issues being separated. Gave this review 2 stars since I got a good laugh out of the whole thing. TIPS Don't go there with good clothes unless you like messing them up. You will be hands and knees crawling. Also if you are like me and have issues with and type of light sensitivity, particularly with strobe lighting, you may want to think hard about if it is worth it. If you can stuff a few bags into one they charge you one fee for bag check (even though that might change after this review)
name: Bane Haunted House

text: If you have to use the bathroom go to the first bathrooms you see because the rest are really far. I would suggest taking the shuttle to the opposite side of the park. If you're not crazy about fried fast food bring your own meal. There's at least 4 places to eat on the zoo grounds. Funnel cake is in Asia not too far from the camel ride. Please buy the the total experience tickets other wise you can't do the best things like the monorail, children's zoo, bug carousel, dinosaur safari or the Gorilla congo. If you get the regular priced tickets you will have to pay extra at these sights. If you're interested there is a ziplining course called Tree Tops on the premises as well. Save the Dino safari for last the line is usually 1 hour plus wait. Be sure to wear comfortable shoes. My feet were dying in my flip flops. text: As I was in NYC for a few days I was desiring to view the city from a skyscraper, and as this place opened not so long time ago I decided to go here ( my other alternatives were Empire State Building and Rockefeller Center ). First of all I checked the pricing in internet, it was closely to the other buildings and you can book them online in order to save some time queuing ( I encourage booking it online ) and see if the time slot has some room left. After that, I headed to the building ( I knew where the queues were ), and there staff asks for if you have a ticket or not ( also the time, as there are open tickets ) and they indicate you the lane you should queue. I waited not more that twenty minutes, it was on time. After getting in the building, you should follow the posters and wait some time. In this lane they will scan your ticket code and then you must pass a security scan ( body and personal objects, like in an airport ). After crossing the security area, it's time to head into the elevators, but in the path you can find some TV screens telling how this building was built and some decoration with rocks. If you think there are no more queues you are wrong, you must queue again to use the elevator that will bring you to the 102nd floor. Elevator's walls will become screens and you will feel like seeing outside the elevator during the time that elevator goes up. In the next area you should wait for all the group and a short film will be played on the wall ( I'm not going to spoil what happens after ). Now you are free to walk, but in this floor you can only find some services ( restaurant, toilets ) and in order to see the views you must use the mechanic stairs to go downstairs. The views are pretty impressive, you can see very far from there. It's not very close to Empire state or Rockefeller center, but you can see every building, places or islands. There's also some screens with a member of the staff playing a quiz with the public. Take your DSLR with good lens in case you have one, it's really worth it. By the end, you should queue in order to use the elevator to go to the first floor, but you can stay as long as you want there.
name: One World Observatory

text: The price is right for this small but lovely zoo. They do a wonderful job of making it feel as natural as it can, with animals which are far out of reach but easily viewable. My only criticism is the price point of everything in the cafe--needs to be about a dollar less for everything in there. Also, once you are inside they do not do a good job at all of ensuring everyone is wearing their masks. I watched twice as Security personnel loped along, looked at whole families with their masks around their necks, and didn't say a word to them about putting their masks back on or keeping a 6-foot minimum distance. They need to get on that.
name: Prospect Park Zoo

text: This is the 1st Hall of Fame in history. Think about that for a minute... Of all the Hall of Fames there are... Basketball Hall of Fame (Springfield, MA), Football Hall of Fame (Canton, Ohio) etc, etc... In the most randomest place, a community college. (Who would have thought!?!) When you look at its history, Bronx Community College was once the northern campus of New York University. NYU's history and influence are still present on the campus. It can be seen in the campus' architecture and spirit. The Hall of Fame is well maintained and its a lovely step back in history for those who would like to learn something new. Show your ID at the gate, sign in and enjoy another gem of the Bronx. Walk around the campus and enjoy the amazing architecture of Marcel Breuer and Stanford White. It's worth a view!
name: The Hall of Fame for Great Americans

text: We attended for a baby dedication and the church is beautiful with a vintage gold design. The church is huge with lots of seating on the lower level and there was even a balcony. The choir was amazing! There had to be at least 100 beautiful voices singing the most familiar gospel songs making it easy to join in and praise with them. Large screens on the left and right of the main stage displayed the words of the music in the event you didn’t know the words. The Choir sang about 3-4 songs while we all stood. Then they sang another 2 songs while we sat. The church was pretty cold for a March day. I never took my off and my daughter kept her coat zipped with her hood on the whole time. There was a guest pastor from Harlem who not resides in Kentucky and his story was very engaging but also a great testimony to Gods love.
name: The Brooklyn Tabernacle

text: I was disappointed with my visit. In my opinion an important historical landmark has not been adequately restored. The only part of the house restored to some form of its original use is the second floor but the restoration did not even encompass the entire second story living space. Three third floor which was the bedroom areas had not been restored and is office space for the employees and is not open to the public. In one area on the second floor that was not restored I felt a rotten floor board break as I walked through the carpeted room. It shows a lack of upkeep and deterioration of the house from lack of care in my opinion. I felt the displays on the first floor were lacking and not very interesting. The wait can be a bear. Only 6 people are allowed to tour the second floor at a time and no time limit is given so if you have slow visitors ahead of an annoying visitor that wants to ask the park rangers a bunch of questions you may be waiting a while. I'm still glad I went but I wish the house was more cared for and expanded for public view.
name: Alexander Hamilton Grange National Memorial

text: The Shops serve as eyecandy for business people on lunch break,tourists, as well as high school students whether they can afford anything or not. Always an interesting mix of people coming through. My tips as a frugal teenager is 1. Sign up for Godiva's reward system so you can claim a free chocolate every month. They still put it in a gold bag so treat yourself and enjoy your complimentary truffle. 2. Check out William's & Sonoma. They always have cute kitchenware. Also, they serve samples of whatever's brewing in the kitchen. You can help yourself to a cup of water and in the winter/fall time they offer small servings of hot cocoa and apple cider. 3.The 2nd floor is used as promotional space sometimes. I got my hair done for free along with free products because garnier & fructis were doing a public event. As I'm typing this, I'm actually at a Google PLUS promo space, working my way towards a pair of GOOGLE SOCKS. Don't judge me. 4. Have somewhere to go and you're looking bleh? Stop by Sephora and give yourself a manicure or do your makeup without being pressured to buy something. 5. I forget how long this place is open until but I always come here to use the bathrooms because it beats a fast food restaurant's. 6. Pretend you're fancy as hell, walk into a designer store and pretend you're trying to figure out what you shall be purchasing for your exclusive event. Pretend you're famous, build an alter ego! Have quite the time *raises monocle*
name: The Shops at Columbus Circle

text: There are always so many complaints about port authority, but for what it is, it does the job, especially if you manage your expectation. Yes, its confusing but follow the signs and use the information display things to look up your gate (if local/nj transit) If you are going on a long-distance bus trip, here are some tips for you --- make sure you print out your bus tickets in advance - eat or grab food outside of port authority - there are so many more options and cheaper than eating inside. - if you need to buy water, soda, snacks, buy them at duane reade outside the station or just inside. Much cheaper than the kiosks inside. - Give yourself enough time to get there and get in line - just because you have a ticket doesn't mean you will get on the bus you are expecting. This is not the airplane with assigned seats. If the bus fills up before you get there, you will get bumped to the next bus. - Tag team if you can on going to the bathroom. Especially in the evening, they are not pleasant and you really dont want to take your bags in, if you don't have to. Another alternative -- if you have time for a drink before your trip, have one at heartland brewery and use the bathroom there before you head in to the station.
name: Port Authority Midtown Bus Terminal

text: Happiness is a very simple term which is used commonly. Even a small kid can tell the meaning of happiness. But how many of us really know the meaning of true happiness and how to attain that state? Not many! Most people look for happiness outside. They believe that they can be happy if they possess certain things or be with certain people or reach a professional height. This is what they have been fed with since their childhood. While all the mentioned things are essential for a good living, they cannot bring happiness. Happiness is something that only you can bring for yourself. If you choose to be happy and channelize for thoughts accordingly, then you shall attain happiness. However, it is not as simple as it seems. You need to make efforts to work on it. Secondly, it is not a one-time activity. You need to practice certain things daily in order to achieve this state. Now, while you need to look for happiness inside at times you need to seek help from your family and friends. Many people these days suffer from depression because they choose to deal with their problems on their own and not to involve others. This is wrong! It is important to look within to find true happiness but it is equally important to surround yourself with positive people.
name: Statue of Liberty National Monument

text: A great experience from beginning to end. My wife and I were in town for our honeymoon and purposely saved this for the night before we left. We wanted to be up there for sunset so I looked at the ticket options. $35 for regular admission, OK. $45 for priority, better. $55 for priority, wider time range (4pm – 8pm) PLUS $15 to spend on food, drinks or gift shop, seriously?? How does anyone get any ticket other than the $55 one? Value for the dollar isn’t even close. We arrived around 7pm and were quickly directed into the priority line past everyone else (guess the value thing doesn’t register with everyone). We were at the top within 5 – 8 minutes. No kidding. Once there we took in the incredible views and then decided to have a snack at the café. The chocolate chip muffins were absolutely incredible!! And, well, free essentially. We spent our total of $30 in vouchers, watched the sunset, took in the view of the city after sunset and then watched everyone crowd toward the exit around 9pm. Once again entered the priority line and were literally at the bottom within 2 minutes of saying “OK, let’s head down”. Seriously people, I don’t mind saying it because I’ll likely never head up there again, but the $55 option is really only $40 (as long as you intended to spend money on things at the top anyway), so for $5 extra you get to skip all of the lines and get a cool iPad thing at the top that points out buildings and sights that you can see plus includes some audio info if you choose.
name: One World Observatory

text: A sensory immersion experience to delight most. As you walk through the experience, each room is a different depth and habitant of the Pacific ocean. In some rooms there are things to touch, or see, or listen to. This provides an exceptional education on our oceans and delivers an important message about the fragility of it's ecosystem/food webs. The only reason I didn't give it 5 is because the experience may exclude certain people. If you get motion sickness I throw a word of caution, some rooms may have you spinning. If you or your child have sensory sensitivities, such as individuals with Autism, you may not be able to handle the extreme changes in light and occasional, very loud noises.
name: National Geographic Encounter

text: Disappointing for several reasons. First of all I have visited dozens of churches in Europe in the US just in the last eight weeks and this is the first one where I was actually charged admission. Normally it is $10 but they only charged 5 because the church is under so much construction. Second, by its nature the church is a "democratic" church so there are no pews and far fewer decorations than in most churches. While it is apparently the longest Church in the US or even the world it is largely unadorned except for the blue rose window in the back. So the combination of an admission fee and very little to actually look at made this a disappointing visit.
name: The Cathedral Church of St. John the Divine

text: Dec 2020. Timed entry is meaningless. The rink is extremely crowded & it is impossible to socially distance, especially in the skate rental/locker area. Inadequate guards on the ice plainly evident by sheer number of skaters staring at their phones or recording videos out on the ice (in the past I’ve seen staff quick to stop this). Many people not wearing masks, including some staff (!) and it’s impossible to get away from their breath - you can see it in the cold air and there’s just not enough space. On top of that there are too many dangerous, daredevil skaters who are not reined in by staff. On my most recent visit I witnessed a woman totally plowed into and head smashed on the ice. She was taken away by EMS. The young man who did it was asked to leave, but his friends didn’t learn or didn’t care, and just kept the antics going. Not 10 min later a family with a young child was next, slammed against the wall like bowling pins when the offender lost his balance and went flying. I don’t think I will be visiting again unless it’s under new management or a private party.

text: The Statue of Liberty is a must visit for anyone on vacation or even if you live in the area and have never gotten around to visiting. You can get to liberty island from both the New Jersey and New York sides of the Hudson. You’ll have to buy tickets for the ferry and also get a ticket if you want to go to the crown of the Statue of Liberty. I personally like visiting from the NJ side because you can also go to Ellis Island and see that historical site too. Although, I believe you can get tickets to do both the statue and Ellis from the New York Side, but I’ve never done it. It’s something to behold standing on Liberty Island looking up at the Statue of Liberty. The island itself isn't that large, but it has never seemed too crowded the times we've visited and has always been relatively clean. In addition to the statue, it’s also a great site to turn around and look at lower manhattan. While it’s still jarring not to see the twin towers standing there, it’s an amazing view seeing Freedom Tower raising above the skyline. On a busy day it’s amazing to look at all the activity in the Hudson. You'll see badges, ferris, cruise ships, small boats, tug boats, and more. This a a great place to visit for both the young and the old. If you’ve never visited, make sure you do someday, you won't regret it.
name: Statue of Liberty National Monument

Great memory!

text: Oh my god this is the worst experience I ever had in any sphere of customer service. First of all Marcus Castillo person that works there is the rudest man I ever met, second he doesn’t speak English enough to understand the message you’re conveying to him, neither is he able to explain anything himself. Today he literally kept my car hostage because I lost the ticket. He turned on my car’s lights and continue to help other customers without giving me an explanation on what he’s going to do now that I cannot provide him with the ticket. I paid in advance through ParkWhiz and wanted to explain that to him, but he wouldn’t listen. “No ticket no car” he kept on saying. When I asked for his manager he said “no manager, the ticket is manager”!!! He was rude, kept on walking aways from me without any explanation. I felt so awful and couldn’t believe someone had just behaved like that in place of business. If the owner wants to keep his customers he should really think about who’s he hiring.... worst experience of my life. Will never go back there!!!

text: Don't let the name fool you. It's not a typical library. You can sign up for a membership on the spot, which is free, and from that point, you check out small sketchbooks to browse art. The art itself is made by regular people and you have the choice yourself of buying a sketchbook to contribute. This is an international project, so you will find yourself exploring the anonymous or eponymous work of a stranger across the country or across the world. The artwork is full of variety. You may run across something inspiring and others may look like student projects. No matter, it's a good way to spend a few hours while walking around in the neighborhood
name: Brooklyn Art Library

text: We went on a sunny Saturday. Got the ferry over around 1pm which was easy with very little queuing. We walked around the fort and the castle which were both interesting. We then hired bikes from Blazing Saddles and cycled around for a couple of hours. It's great that there are cycle paths everywhere and points to lock your bike up (like when you want to check out the view of Manhattan from the top of the Outlook Hill, which is so worth it). There are plenty of food options in the center of the island. We enjoyed some fresh organic ice cream. Only issue was the queue for the ferry to get back. I think because so many people leave around the same time to get home (5pm). Anticipate a wait if leaving the island early evening.

text: BikeLane is my local bike store, and I love coming here for help on my fixie. They sell inexpensive, high quality, high performance bikes and parts for any bike. The store is very clean and aesthetic, but what brings it to life is their awesome and lively crew who are quite funny and always have a smile on their faces; they want to assist you in finding anything that would make you extremely happy with your bicycle and take pride in their work and shop. In the past I've gone to other bike shops full of salesmen looking to sell you whatever (usually outside my budget) . The crew at BikeLane was looking into helping me choose the right tire for me within my price range. I spoke to Franky and he recommended a tire that he uses himself which not only gives extra protection to my inner tube, but also has a reflective lining on the sides so drivers can see me at night with ease, and honestly I couldn't be happier with my purchase! I salute you guys
name: BikeLane Bicycle Store
category: Bicycle Shop||Bicycle club||Bicycle rack||Bicycle repair shop||Bicycle wholesale||Electric motor store||Parking lot for bicycles||Repair service||Scooter repair shop||Skate shop

text: I first came here back in 2016 for the free kayaking. It was a blazing hot day and I just HAD to find a way to get out on the water, somewhere in the city. Manhattan Community Boathouse was just the place! I felt so welcomed and had so much fun during my first visit that I decided to become a volunteer. 5 years later, I still spend almost every summer weekend here, helping the organization offer the public the priceless (FREE!) experience of paddling on the Hudson river. Sharing this unique experience with the public is one of my favorite things to do in the city, and seeing the smiles on people's faces makes it all worth it. As a volunteer, I enjoy the extra training classes offered and volunteer-only kayaking trips we get to go on as well! A few things to know for the 2021 season, some of which is different from prior year reviews: MCBH is operating by reservation to prevent overcrowding. They are open Saturdays and Sundays from 12 PM - 6 PM through October 10. You can reserve your spot up to 2 weeks in advance on the website. Paddlers of all skill levels are invited to participate as long as you know how to swim. Anyone under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a parent or guardian. They offer changing rooms and lockers with locks provided, so you can bring a change of clothes if you want, because you will get wet. There's no public restroom here though, so be sure to stop in Clinton Dewitt park at 54th & 11th Ave before walking over if you need to use the facilities! Otherwise, a portapotty along the bike path near the boathouse is the only toilet option. Did I mention it's all free? The program is run entirely by volunteers, and funded solely by donations from the public, but there is absolutely no pressure to donate. Whether you come as a volunteer or as a member of the public, I think this is the best free activity in NYC!
name: Manhattan Community Boathouse - Pier 96

text: The Cloisters has an incredible collection art artwork, tapestries, sculptures, gardens, sarcophagi, etc... from the middle ages. Easily an all day visit. If that's not enough this museum is nestled in the heart of Fort Tyron Park overlooking the Hudson River and the mighty Pallisades beyond. And if that STILL isn't enough the mesuem offers a discount for those who pay the full recommend admission to the affiliated Metropolitan Museum of Art. Plus they've got a cafe.
name: The Met Cloisters

text: One World Trade Center One World Trade Center logo.svg One World Trade Center Fähre.jpg Wikimedia | OpenStreetMap Alternative names 1 WTC Freedom Tower (pre-2009)[1] Record height Tallest in North America since 2013[I] Preceded by Willis Tower General information Status Complete Type OfficeObservationCommunication Architectural style Contemporary modern Location 285 Fulton Street Manhattan, New York City Coordinates 40°42′46.8″N 74°0′48.6″WCoordinates: 40°42′46.8″N 74°0′48.6″W Construction started April 27, 2006 Topped-out May 10, 2013[2] Opened November 3, 2014[3][4] May 29, 2015 (One World Observatory)[5] Cost US$3.9 billiona [6][7] Height Architectural 1,776 ft (541.3 m)[8][9] Tip 1,792 ft (546.2 m)[8] Roof 1,368 ft (417.0 m)[10] Top floor 1,268 ft (386.5 m)[8] Observatory 1,254 ft (382.2 m)[8] Technical details Floor count 94 (+5 below ground floors)[8][11] Floor area 3,501,274 sq ft (325,279 m2)[8] Lifts/elevators 73,[8] made by ThyssenKrupp.[12] Design and construction Architect David Childsb Daniel Libeskind (2002)[13] Developer Port Authority of New York and New Jersey[8] Structural engineer WSP Cantor Seinuk Other designers Hill International, The Louis Berger Group[14] Main contractor Tishman Construction References [8][15] a. April 2012 estimate. b. Skidmore, Owings & Merrill. One World Trade Center (also known as One WTC, 1 World Trade Center, 1 WTC, or Freedom Tower[16]) is the main building of the rebuilt World Trade Center complex in Lower Manhattan, New York City. One WTC is the tallest building in the United States, the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere, and the sixth-tallest in the world. The supertall structure has the same name as the North Tower of the original World Trade Center, which was destroyed in the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001. The new skyscraper stands on the northwest corner of the 16-acre (6.5 ha) World Trade Center site, on the site of the original 6 World Trade Center. The building is bounded by West Street to the west, Vesey Street to the north, Fulton Street to the south, and Washington Street to the east. The building's architect is David Childs, whose firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill (SOM) also designed the Burj Khalifa and the Willis Tower. The construction of below-ground utility relocations, footings, and foundations for the new building began on April 27, 2006. One World Trade Center became the tallest structure in New York City on April 30, 2012, when it surpassed the height of the Empire State Building. The tower's steel structure was topped out on August 30, 2012. On May 10, 2013, the final component of the skyscraper's spire was installed, making the building, including its spire, reach a total height of 1,776 feet (541 m). Its height in feet is a deliberate reference to the year when the United States Declaration of Independence was signed. The building opened on November 3, 2014;[4] the One World Observatory opened on May 29, 2015.[5]
name: One World Trade Center

text: I remember going to this building back in the day when it housed the Whitney Museum of Modern Art. I must say, Met Breuer made a great choice of space to display the modern, innovative, and quirky artwork. The fact that it's very well compartmentalized and relatively small, compared to the Metropolitan Museum, really allows you to enjoy and appreciate all the permanent and special exhibits in a more leisurely, much less touristy environment. If you are a local, or a frequent visitor to NYC, make sure to visit the museum every so often, as the special exhibits are changed often and are very well curated. Personally, I am not a fan and am rather sceptical of abstract art, for example, but some pieces of abstract art Met Breuer showcases don't leave me feeling impartial.
name: The Met Breuer

text: The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) is an art museum located in Midtown Manhattan, New York City, on 53rd Street between Fifth and Sixth Avenues. It plays a major role in developing and collecting modern art, and is often identified as one of the largest and most influential museums of modern art in the world. MoMA's collection offers an overview of modern and contemporary art, including works of architecture and design, drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, prints, illustrated books and artist's books, film, and electronic media. The MoMA Library includes approximately 300,000 books and exhibition catalogs, over 1,000 periodical titles, and over 40,000 files of ephemera about individual artists and groups. The archives hold primary source material related to the history of modern and contemporary art.
name: The Museum of Modern Art

It is the best experience I have had in New York, the warmth of the people who attend the service, the kindness and attention to the visits, the shepherds with their joyful way of sharing, the music, it is amazing to hear those beautiful voices and feel part of the celebration that takes place.
